response: In C# you can measure the time it takes to send a UDP packet by using a stopwatch. This measures the time from when the packet is sent until it is transmitted from the local machine. It does not include the time it takes for the server to receive or respond. You can start the stopwatch before sending the packet and stop it immediately after the send call. This gives you the transmission time on the sending side. You will need to use the System.Diagnostics namespace and the Stopwatch class. This approach does not involve waiting for a response from the server. It only checks the time taken to send the packet from the client.
